qa_pairs.question: What is the recommended approach for migrating data in blocks if Globus is not supported?
qa_pairs.answer: You should make a schedule to migrate your data in blocks of a few hundreds of GBs at a time.

qa_pairs.question: What happens if a data transfer stops when migrating in blocks of a few hundreds of GBs?
qa_pairs.answer: If the transfer stops for some reason, you will be able to try again starting from the incomplete file, but you will not have to re-transfer files that are already complete.

qa_pairs.question: What resource can assist with resuming data transfers if Globus is not used?
qa_pairs.answer: An organized list of files will help when resuming data transfers if Globus is not used.

qa_pairs.question: How can one monitor the progress of a data transfer?
qa_pairs.answer: You should check regularly to see that the transfer process has not stopped. File size is a good indicator of progress.

qa_pairs.question: What is a sign that a data transfer might have gone wrong?
qa_pairs.answer: If no files have changed size for several minutes, then something may have gone wrong.

qa_pairs.question: Who should be contacted if a data transfer stops and restarting it doesn't resolve the issue?
qa_pairs.answer: If restarting the transfer does not work, contact our technical support.
